subject: Product Management
title: Talking to Churned Users
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Talking to Churned Users.

hook.text: Your most valuable users might be the ones who left. They tried your product, saw its promise, and walked away.

buildup.text: Churned users can articulate exactly what was missing — the gap between your promise and their experience.

discovery.text: The question to ask: 'What would have made you stay?' Their answers map directly to your PMF gaps.

twist.text: Most teams focus on acquiring new users. The churned users already trusted you once — they're easier to win back.

climax.text: Closing the gap between why users signed up and why they left is the fastest path to improving PMF.

punchline.text: Ask the people who left why they left. They'll tell you.

quiz.question: Why are churned users valuable for improving PMF?
options: They can articulate exactly what was missing between your promise and their experience
They're more likely to pay premium prices
